title: Contemporary Issues on Adolescents and Counseling
narrative:
Short impact summary

Published in 2024 by Peter Lang Verlag, Contemporary Issues on Adolescents and Counseling by Ruth A. Aderanti and Abiodun M. Gesinde is a recently released scholarly volume in psychology that currently sits in the early stages of its academic life cycle. With four library holdings recorded and no citations yet accrued, the book has a modest but measurable institutional footprint that reflects the typical trajectory of a newly published specialized text.

Full narrative

Authored by Ruth A. Aderanti and Abiodun M. Gesinde, this volume addresses a domain of enduring relevance within psychology: the counseling needs and challenges faced by adolescents in contemporary contexts. Published through Peter Lang Verlag, an established academic press with a long history in the humanities and social sciences, the book enters a field where practitioner guidance, developmental psychology, and applied counseling theory converge. Given the intensifying global attention to adolescent mental health, the subject matter carries considerable professional and social timeliness.

In terms of measurable scholarly impact, the book is in the very earliest phase of circulation. No formal citations have been recorded through available indexing sources, and event-level engagement data remains absent at this stage. This is broadly consistent with the publication timeline — 2024 releases in specialized academic subfields often require a year or more before citation activity begins to register in major databases. Its percentile ranking reflects this nascent status rather than any qualitative judgment about the work's contribution.

Library adoption, while modest, provides one grounded signal of early reach. Four holdings have been recorded across institutions via Open Library, indicating that the volume has begun to enter academic library collections. This kind of institutional acquisition is a meaningful first step for a specialized counseling text, as it positions the book for potential course adoption and reference use among graduate students, counselor educators, and practitioners working with young populations.

Looking ahead, the book's prospects for broader uptake will likely depend on how well it resonates with scholars and practitioners working at the intersection of adolescent development and therapeutic intervention — particularly in contexts where culturally situated perspectives on counseling are valued. As the scholarly conversation around adolescent mental health continues to expand globally, Contemporary Issues on Adolescents and Counseling is positioned to contribute to that discourse, though its full impact remains to be determined as it gains greater exposure in the academic community.
